paper.title: The Gaia RVS benchmark stars II. A sample of stars selected for their Gaia high radial velocity

review.summary: Paper presents a chemical analysis of 43 stars selected for their high Gaia radial velocities (|Vr|>500 km/s). Two LaTeX tables appear in the source: a solar abundance reference table and an observation log of S/N values. Two online tables (C.1 and C.2) are deposited at CDS containing the full stellar parameters, abundances, NLTE corrections, and line lists.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Lists the adopted solar reference abundances A(X) used as the zero-point for the chemical analysis of the programme stars.

columns.name: A(X)
columns.meaning: Solar abundance of the element on the logarithmic scale where A(H)=12

columns.name: Reference
columns.meaning: Literature source from which the solar abundance value was adopted

internal_tables.evidence: Single-column table in the main text (Sec. 4.1) providing solar reference values for C, O, Na, Mg, Al, Si, S, Ca, Sc, Ti, V, Cr, Mn, Fe, Co, Ni, Cu, Zn, Y, Eu, and Ba.
internal_tables.comments: References alternate between Caffau et al. (abbosunEC) and Lodders et al. 2009. This table is explicitly cited in Sec. 4.2 as the solar reference for the abundance analysis.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Records the signal-to-noise ratio per pixel at 600 nm for each programme star and observation period, summarising the data quality of the UVES spectra.

columns.name: Star
columns.meaning: Internal identifier of the programme star (RVS###)

columns.name: Period
columns.meaning: ESO observation period(s) in which the star was observed (109, 110, or 109+110)

columns.name: S/N @600 nm
columns.meaning: Signal-to-noise ratio per pixel at approximately 600 nm for the corresponding observation period; some entries are upper limits or blank

internal_tables.evidence: Single-column table in the main text listing all 46 observed stars (including RVS705 and RVS724 which were not chemically analysed) with their Gaia DR3 IDs, observation periods, and S/N values.
internal_tables.comments: Some stars appear on two rows when observed in both periods (e.g. RVS712, RVS736). RVS724 has a blank S/N. RVS705 is marked as <20. The table includes the two stars (RVS705, RVS724) for which no chemical analysis was performed.

external_resources.description: Online table C.1 deposited at CDS containing, for each star: name, Gaia DR3 identifier, stellar parameters (Teff, logg, xi, [Fe/H]), derived abundances for all investigated elements, and the NLTE corrections discussed in the text.

source_refs.context: The stellar parameters and the abundances derived are provided at CDS in an online table that is described in the appendix.

The abundances are provided in an online table (Table C.1) available at CDS. The table contains the name of the star, the Gaia DR3 identifier, the stellar parameters, the abundances, and the NLTE corrections discussed in the text.

external_resources.evidence: Explicitly stated in the main text (Sec. 4.1) and in Appendix C that stellar parameters and abundances are provided in an online table at CDS.
external_resources.comments: No specific CDS identifier or DOI given in the arXiv preprint. Not downloaded during review or extraction.

external_resources.description: Online table C.2 deposited at CDS listing, for each star and element, the individual spectral lines used in the star-by-star analysis with their atomic data, including wavelength and log gf values.

source_refs.context: The lines used in the star-by-star analysis for all elements are provided at CDS in an online table (Table C.2). We recall that MyGIsFOS performed a fit in chosen wavelength ranges. In the table, all the lines of the designated elements presented in the range are reported.

external_resources.evidence: Explicitly stated in Appendix C that the line lists used for the chemical analysis are provided in an online table at CDS.
external_resources.comments: No specific CDS identifier or DOI given in the arXiv preprint. Not downloaded during review or extraction.
